screens.name: Harmony evidence extraction workflow
screens.purpose: Produce inspectable, non-authoritative measurements for an agent to adjudicate.

screens.name: Musician adjudication workflow
screens.purpose: Turn measured hypotheses into form-aware chord decisions while preserving alternatives.

ux_rules: Present evidence extraction before musician adjudication and never imply that numeric rank equals a final chord.
Keep evidence support, decision confidence, and external corroboration as separate provenance layers.
Expose half-time, nominal, and double-time metrical hypotheses when plausible rather than hiding octave ambiguity.
Require the agent to state why a selected label explains bass motion, local key, phrase function, and recurrence peers.
Use actionable diagnostics for missing tools and optional dependencies.
Keep generated output compact enough for an agent to compare candidates without re-reading raw frame data.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Use staged JSON artifacts with concise summaries and raw evidence references.
interaction_risks: Treating the highest template score as authoritative.
Suppressing half-time or double-time tempo alternatives.
Allowing a vocal-dominated full mix to outweigh a harmonic source.
Emitting excessive frame-level data that obscures comparison.
notes: No rendered product surface is changed.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Keep decisions adjacent to candidates, evidence references, alternatives, and recurrence actions.
interaction_risks: Conflating evidence support with decision confidence.
Over-labeling extensions detected only in vocals.
Forcing repeated slots to match despite genuine variation.
Using an external chart as ground truth.

constraints: Name the reusable tool extract_harmony_evidence.py rather than analyze_harmony.py.
Never emit chosen_chord, final confidence, final form, or a finished chord chart from the evidence extractor.
Set do_not_select_automatically to true at artifact and segment levels.
Use evidence_support naming for numeric candidate scores.
Retain at least two candidates for every emitted segment.
Apply a documented complexity penalty to seventh, extended, altered, and slash candidates unless stable source support exists.
Keep Demucs outside the extractor and accept optional harmonic and bass source paths.
Bound output size by aggregating over requested segment resolution.
open_decisions: Agent-selected downbeat and form coordinates remain outside the extractor.

constraints: Require explicit metrical-level and beat-grid selection before final beat-positioned harmony.
Require functional and recurrence reasoning for low- and medium-confidence decisions.
Keep external corroboration in a separate field with URL and agreement or conflict notes.
Preserve unresolved harmony rather than inventing precision.

critical_judgments: The script measures rather than adjudicates.
Tempo and beat-grid ambiguity remains visible.
Full mix and harmonic-source evidence remain distinguishable.
Complexity penalties prevent automatic extension inflation.
must_not_regress: Module-mode approved form coordinates remain compatible.
The final analysis validator remains backward compatible.

critical_judgments: The agent owns final chord and form decisions.
Evidence support is not presented as final confidence.
Recurrence is advisory and permits real variation.
External charts cannot silently override audio.
must_not_regress: Bass motion, cadence, and phrase function remain first-class evidence.

visual_constraints: Not applicable: the task changes a non-visual skill, contracts, scripts, and tests only.
